en.extract:
The Javan white-eye (Zosterops flavus) is a bird species in the family Zosteropidae that occurs in Java and Borneo. It is threatened by trapping for wildlife trade and has been assessed as Endangered on the IUCN Red List in 2019, as the wild population declined significantly.
In West Java, it has been recorded in Pulau Dua, Muara Gembong and Tanjung Sedari between 1984 and 1985. Its natural habitat includes tropical moist lowland and mangrove forests and shrubland.
In August 2015, it was sighted in four urban parks of Malang in East Java. Between July 2015 and August 2016, it was also recorded as one of 15 songbird species traded in high volume and with high prices during surveys in wildlife markets in Kalimantan. Shop owners admitted that demand for and value of Javan white-eye has increased.


References


External links
BirdLife International (2019). "Javan White-eye Zosterops flavus".

The '''Javan white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops flavus''''') is a [[bird]] species in the family [[Zosteropidae]] that occurs in [[Java (Indonesia)|Java]] and [[Borneo]]. It is threatened by [[trapping]] for wildlife trade and has been assessed as [[Endangered species|Endangered]] on the [[IUCN Red List]] in 2019, as the wild population declined significantly.<ref name="iucn status 16 November 2021" />

In West Java, it has been recorded in Pulau Dua, Muara Gembong and Tanjung Sedari between 1984 and 1985. Its natural [[habitat]] includes [[Tropical forest|tropical]] moist lowland and [[mangrove]] forests<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Allport |first1=G. |last2=Milton|first2=R. |date=1988 |title=A note on the recent sighting of ''Zosterops flava'' Javan White-eye |journal=Kukila |volume=3 |pages=142–149 |url=https://www.researchgate.net/publication/299394917}}</ref> and [[shrubland]].<ref name="iucn status 16 November 2021" />

In August 2015, it was sighted in four urban parks of [[Malang]] in [[East Java]].<ref>{{cite book |author=Ahmad, M. |author2=Al Muzakki, F. |author3=Abrori, R. |name-list-style=amp |year=2016 |title=Proceedings of International Conference on Natural Mathematical and Environmental Sciences for Sustainable Development |location=Banjarbaru |publisher=Faculty of Mathematics and Natural Sciences |chapter=Study on Bird Diversity in Four City Parks of Malang City, East Java, Indonesia |pages=22–29 |editor1-last=Sari |editor1-first=S. G. |editor2-last=Lestia |editor2-first=A. S. |editor3-last=Sasmita |editor3-first=R. }}{{Dead link|date=March 2023 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> Between July 2015 and August 2016, it was also recorded as one of 15 [[songbird]] species traded in high volume and with high prices during surveys in wildlife markets in [[Kalimantan]]. Shop owners admitted that demand for and value of Javan white-eye has increased.<ref>{{cite journal |title=A Silent Morning: The Songbird Trade in Kalimantan, Indonesia |author=Rentschlar, K. A. |author2=Miller, A. E. |author3=Lauck, K. S. |author4=Rodiansyah, M. |author5=Bobby, Muflihati |author6=Kartikawati |name-list-style=amp |journal=Tropical Conservation Science |year=2018 |volume=11 |article-number=1940082917753909 |doi=10.1177/1940082917753909 |doi-access=free }}</ref>
